title: StrategyItem
description:
A written strategy insight (join-spine `regime_key` row: strategy
write ‚Üî retrieval, identical at both ends). S6
(`role-strategy-memory`) owns retrieval/promotion policy; this type
only carries the join key + content.

schema.description:
Per-kind schema version, bumped on any breaking field change to
that kind (design D2; `canon fmt`/`canon migrate`, S11, key off
it).

Actor.description:
Who/what produced a record ‚Äî structured, never a bare `by: String`
(design D2, the donor audit's biggest cross-family gap).

`role` is `Option` (S11 design D5, "actor backfill is best-effort
from adjacent fields; absent stays absent"): a migrated legacy
record whose only source field is a bare `by: "legacy-ci-machine"`
string carries no role information at all ‚Äî `canon migrate` maps
that string to `agent_id` and leaves `role: null` rather than
guessing, so the field must be able to express "unknown", not just
"known". Every record canon itself originates still supplies a role
via [`Actor::new`]; only backfilled/migrated records use
[`Actor::new_unattributed`].

RecordKind.description:
The thirteen closed record kinds `canon-model` recognizes (design D1;
`Subject` is the reviewed 13th kind, added by s36 ‚Äî the "a new kind is
a reviewed, breaking `canon-model` change" process design D1 mandates,
exercised for real). A fourteenth kind is again a reviewed, breaking
change ‚Äî never a `kind: String` escape hatch (design D1's explicitly
rejected alternative: an untyped `payload: serde_json::Value` would
silently recreate the "no documented join key" problem inside canon
itself).

RegimeKey.description: `<role>/<repo>/<area>/<hash>`, four `/`-separated segments, the last a lowercase-hex digest ‚Äî joins: strategy write ‚Üî retrieval (identical at both ends)

RoleId.description: kebab-case role slug (`[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`), e.g. `implementer`, `reviewer` ‚Äî joins: not a join-spine key; used by `Actor.role` and `regime_key`'s `<role>` segment

SessionId.description: opaque, non-empty agent-CLI session token; derivation source varies by adapter (Claude Code/Codex: transcript filename stem; omp/pi: in-file header `id` field) ‚Äî this type validates shape only, never a single fixed format ‚Äî joins: session ‚Üî cost ‚Üî run ‚Üî trajectory

TaskId.description: `<change_id>#<n>`, where `<n>` is one or more dot-separated integers (`1`, `1.1`, `6.2`) matching `tasks.md`'s own numbering ‚Äî joins: task ‚Üî evidence ‚Üî trajectory
